action.reasoning: Observation: Best accuracy is 80.32% from iteration 4 (ConvNeXt + mixup/LS/EMA). Cleanlab (iter 7) and self-training (iter 6) both failed (~60% val_acc). Time remaining: ~30 min. Analysis: The simple regularization approach works best. To beat 80.32%, ensembling two diverse models is the most promising approach. I'll train a new ConvNeXt-Tiny model from scratch with different seed/hyperparams for 12 epochs (~18 min), then ensemble with the existing best.pt in predict.py. Submitting eval with best.pt as primary (guaranteed fallback to 80.32%) and best_v2.pt as secondary for ensembling.
